mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, which triggers risk-off positioning and compresses equity valuations within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal exhibits no predictive power for weekly drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising residential mortgage debt indicates expanding household balance sheets and credit availability, which fuels consumption-driven earnings growth and lifts equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 12-month rolling window, or the correlation between housing credit expansion and corporate revenue growth breaks down.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions where industrial production exceeds trend growth, supply constraints tighten profit margins for cyclical equities, causing the S&P 500 to rally on earnings revision momentum before reverting.
candidate_trigger: regime: late-cycle expansion with capacity constraints
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity earnings revisions, or the signal generates negative forward returns during confirmed late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands the denominator for corporate earnings, producing a linear positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with quarterly equity returns across multiple business cycles, or earnings per share growth diverges persistently from output growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing central banks to signal tighter policy and depressing equity valuations within five trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally following CPI spikes above the threshold, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-inflation and low-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive equity returns when payroll growth exceeds 200,000 and unemployment falls below 5.5%, or the signal produces negative forward returns during confirmed labor market expansions.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis credit expansion phases, monthly housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer confidence and durable goods demand, generating short-term positive equity returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to lead consumer spending or equity returns during credit expansion periods, or the signal produces negative forward returns when starts exceed historical trend levels.
